title: For the choirmaster. A Maskil of David. After Doeg the Edomite went to Saul and told him, “David has gone to the house of Ahimelech.”

verses.1: Why do you boast of evil, O mighty man? The loving devotion of God endures all day long.
verses.2: Your tongue devises destruction like a sharpened razor, O worker of deceit.
verses.3: You love evil more than good, falsehood more than speaking truth. Selah
verses.4: You love every word that devours, O deceitful tongue.
verses.5: Surely God will bring you down to everlasting ruin; He will snatch you up and tear you away from your tent; He will uproot you from the land of the living. Selah
verses.6: The righteous will see and fear; they will mock the evildoer, saying,
verses.7: “Look at the man who did not make God his refuge, but trusted in the abundance of his wealth and strengthened himself by destruction.”
verses.8: But I am like an olive tree flourishing in the house of God; I trust in the loving devotion of God forever and ever.
verses.9: I will praise You forever, because You have done it. I will wait on Your name—for it is good—in the presence of Your saints.

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
